original_hypothesis.title: Staking Pools Rendered Inoperable by Validator Whitelist Enforcement
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When the validator whitelist is enabled, EdgeChallengeManager enforces that msg.sender is a whitelisted validator. Because staking pools are deployed permissionlessly via CREATE2, they are not whitelisted by default, causing all pool-initiated edge creations to revert.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Staking pools are intended to allow permissionless aggregation of funds. If the core protocol's whitelist mechanism blocks contract-based aggregators, the primary mechanism for retail participation is broken.
deployment_assumptions: Admin does not manually whitelist every dynamically created pool contract
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: RollupAdminLogic.sol: setValidatorWhitelistDisabled controls the flag; EdgeChallengeManager.sol:134 enforces it on msg.sender.
preconditions: Validator whitelist is enabled on the Rollup
Users deposit funds into an EdgeStakingPool
The pool attempts to create an edge

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: The whitelist checks msg.sender, which is the pool contract, not the user initiating the transaction.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires admin to have enabled the whitelist.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time the whitelist is active.

operational_questions: Is there a mechanism to automatically whitelist staking pools created by the official factory?
Are staking pools intended to be used only when the whitelist is disabled?
validation_requirements: Deploy a pool, enable whitelist, and attempt to call createEdge.

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Admin enables validator whitelist for security reasons.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Users continue to deploy and fund staking pools.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: When a pool reaches the required threshold, any call to createEdge reverts.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: User funds are stuck in the pool because the protocol move cannot be executed, and withdrawal may be blocked if the pool logic expects the move to complete.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Complete failure of the trustless staking pool ecosystem.
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Admin must manually whitelist all pool contracts or disable the whitelist.

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Reverted createEdge transactions with NotValidator error.
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Fully funded pools failing to create edges.

validation_result.mechanism: Validator whitelist blocks staking pool edge creation
validation_result.impact_summary: When the validator whitelist is enabled, EdgeStakingPool contracts cannot call createLayerZeroEdge because the whitelist check validates msg.sender (the pool contract), which is not whitelisted, rendering the permissionless edge staking pool system non-functional.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The hypothesis correctly identifies an architectural incompatibility between the validator whitelist mechanism in EdgeChallengeManager and the permissionless EdgeStakingPool system.

In EdgeChallengeManager.createLayerZeroEdge (line ~133-136), the whitelist check validates msg.sender:
